pick.rationale: Purdue enters as the clear favorite in this 2 vs 7 seed matchup, ranking #8 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with the nation's #1 offense that should exploit Miami's solid but not elite defense ranked 45th. The efficiency gap is substantial, with Purdue's +30.5 adjusted efficiency margin exceeding Miami's +20.67 by nearly 10 points, reflecting a significant talent and quality disparity. While Miami demonstrated tournament viability by defeating Missouri in the Round of 64 and boasts a respectable 25-8 record from the ACC, Purdue's Big Ten pedigree, elite offensive firepower, and historical 2-seed success rate in the Round of 32 provide a decisive edge in this West Region contest.

reasoningStep.title: Purdue vs Miami (FL) Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Purdue holds a significant advantage with the nation's #1 offense and roughly 10-point efficiency margin gap over Miami, making them the clear favorite in this 2 vs 7 seed matchup despite Miami's solid ACC pedigree.
reasoningStep.evidence: Purdue ranks #8 nationally (KenPom/Torvik) with +30.5 adj EM and #1 offense (132.55 adjOE in Torvik, #2 in KenPom) vs Miami's #30-35 ranking with +20.67 adj EM and #34-35 offense. Historical 2-seed vs 7-seed Round of 32 win rate is approximately 70-75%. Both teams won their Round of 64 games, but Purdue's 90% confidence victory over Queens demonstrates their dominance while Miami's 62% win over Missouri was more competitive.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://www.palmbeachpost.com/story/sports/college/hurricanes/2026/03/15/miami-purdue-march-madness-espn-jay-bilas/89173205007/ and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: ESPN analyst says Miami can challenge Purdue in NCAA tournament 2026 Skip to main content Home Heat Dolphins Marlins High School Fishing Hurricanes Gators Seminoles Owls Golf USA TODAY Sports SportsbookWire Jay Bilas said Purdue's March Madness path was easy. What about Miami? Dan Rorabaugh USA TODAY NETWORK - Florida Show Caption Hide Caption Who’s ready to put on the glass slipper this March? Two underdog teams that could make a Cinderella run this March Madness are Saint Louis and Miami (Ohio). Bracketologists seemed to be writing off Miami after their fate was revealed in the 2026 NCAA men's basketball tournament . At least one ESPN analyst came to the Hurricanes' defense. The Canes received a No. 7 seed in the West Region and will face No. 10 Missouri in what's effectively a road game in their first-round matchup in St. Louis . If they win to continue their March Madness expedition, Miami would likely get No. 2 Purdue in the second round (unless 15-seed Queens University pulls off a massive upset). The CBS selection show didn't spend much time discussing UM and its prospects in the bracket, perhaps because their lead-in was the Boilermakers knocking off Michigan to win the Big Ten tournament championship. And it seemed ESPN's Bracketology show would go on the same route. Buy Miami NCAA tournament tickets Jay Bilas got the first shot at analyzing the West Region and while he thought top-seeded Arizona had a tough draw, he felt differently about the bottom half of the bracket. "I think Purdue has a pretty decent draw here," Bilas said. In fairness, he did go on to say "clearly Miami-Missouri, that winner's not going to be an easy second-round game" before expounding on the weaknesses of No. 3-seed Gonzaga and No. 6 BYU. But then former North Carolina guard Jay Williams stepped in to challenge the Duke alum. "I think Purdue will be OK, but Miami's a really good team," Williams said. "Miami's played well. Jai Lucas has done one heck of a job with this team, obviously Tre Donaldson is a guy who can knock down shots, you think about Shelton Henderson, a guy that can attack ..." "And (Malik) Reneau is a matchup problem," Seth Greenberg chimed in before Williams talked up Ernest Udeh Jr.'s abilities on both ends. "So yes, I do like Purdue, but that could be a good matchup against Trey Kaufman-Renn and how they like to dominate the boards," Williams concluded. Miami has had an excellent season under its first-year coach, going from 7-24 to 25-8 and reaching the ACC tournament semifinals. The Hurricanes beat North Carolina when both Caleb Wilson and Seth Trimble (big man Henri Veesaar was out with an injury). And they've got two All-ACC players in Reneau and Donaldson. Here's hoping Purdue doesn't have a repeat of 2023, when they became the second No. 1 seed to lose in the first round.
